title: skopeo copy sets the wrong mediaType
body:
The "scopeo copy" subcommand sets the wrong mediaType in the manifest.json when copying an image from docker daemon.

Reproduction steps:

```bash
$ skopeo --version
skopeo version 1.20.0 commit: e2c1eecd40b9121adf431a33cbbe60d22dc9fad7
```

Dockerfile
```Dockerfile
FROM scratch

ADD Dockerfile /
```

```bash
$ docker build --tag test:latest .
$ skopeo copy docker-daemon:test:latest dir:tmp
$ cat tmp/manifest.json | jq
{
  "schemaVersion": 2,
  "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json",
  "config": {
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.container.image.v1+json",
    "size": 443,
    "digest": "sha256:b74fe97ecf331e9e891b5dd69aa116fe864e14df52d11383062f3b8776ef35b0"
  },
  "layers": [
    {
      "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.image.rootfs.diff.tar.gzip",
      "size": 2048,
      "digest": "sha256:de9281e264b8760ca2e7a84665c3cab92e474b6ff5955568d70c20b1aa9a9771"
    }
  ]
}
$ file tmp/de9281e264b8760ca2e7a84665c3cab92e474b6ff5955568d70c20b1aa9a9771 
tmp/de9281e264b8760ca2e7a84665c3cab92e474b6ff5955568d70c20b1aa9a9771: POSIX tar archive
```

The "mediaType" is set to "application/vnd.docker.image.rootfs.diff.tar.gzip", but the file is tar archive without gzip compression.
The correct "mediaType" would be "application/vnd.docker.image.rootfs.diff.tar"

Using the "--dest-compress" option is a workaround, but the option is not supported for remote docker:// copy.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

The decision to use a gzip MIME type for the uncompressed contents is tracked in https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/246 .

But the behavior is somewhat `dir:`-specific, `dir:` prefers not to change the compression of the input. I’d expect that, in _most_ situations (= when the registry doesn’t already store the uncompressed version), a copy from `docker-demon` to a registry would actually compress the layer. (To be reliable, Skopeo would need `ForceCompressionFormat` to be exposed as a CLI option.)

Is that consistent with what you observe?
can close this as duplicate and comment in https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/246 instead. Hope it'll progress
